raw_text:

{
  "claim_texts": [
    "15% of California households could afford to buy a $843,600 median-priced single-family home in late 2023.",
    "Homeownership rates statewide in California have declined over the years.",
    "Roughly 55% of California homes were owned by their occupants in 2022, down from about 60% in 2006.",
    "The homeownership rate for Californians aged 35 to 45 declined from about 49% to nearly 40% between 2000 to 2021.",
    "Corporate ownership of homes surged in the wake of the Great Recession due to subprime mortgage foreclosures.",

    "Some firms own thousands of single-family homes in California, targeting areas prone to higher rent growth.",
    "Institutional investors are able to outbid working families for single-family homes by using their wealth to purchase properties in cash.",
    "Institutional investors are buying smaller, modest homes that would typically be purchased by first-time homebuyers.",
    "Properties owned by Invitation Homes are concentrated in regions with low housing growth and increasing demand."
